burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL impact and social stigma

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical history of urgency with leakage; bladder diary; rule out UTI

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with behavioral therapy and meds

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care assessment; urology/urogynecology referral as needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bladder training, pelvic floor therapy, antimuscarinics or beta-3 agonists; address comorbidities
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to pelvic floor therapy and specialty care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Behavioral interventions feasible in primary care
